records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §11.2, does your Software Development Plan:
records.query: Describe how the software is organized and built — the life cycle, the processes, and who is responsible?

records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §11.2, does your Software Development Plan:
records.query: Define the transition criteria between phases (how you know one activity is complete enough to start the next)?

records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §11.2, does your Software Development Plan:
records.query: Identify the development environment — the programming languages, compilers, and tools you will use?

records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §11.2, does your Software Development Plan:
records.query: Reference the standards the project will follow?

records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §11.3, does your Software Verification Plan:
records.query: Describe how you will verify the software — the reviews, analyses, and tests — and who performs them independently where your software level requires it?

records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §11.3, does your Software Verification Plan:
records.query: Define the verification environment and the criteria for when verification is complete?

records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §11.4, does your Software Configuration Management Plan:
records.query: Describe what is placed under configuration control, and at what points (the baselines)?

records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §11.4, does your Software Configuration Management Plan:
records.query: Describe problem reporting, change control, and change review?

records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §11.4, does your Software Configuration Management Plan:
records.query: Cover archive, retrieval, release, and how the software is loaded and protected?

records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §11.5, does your Software Quality Assurance Plan:
records.query: Describe how QA will gain assurance that the processes and their outputs follow your plans and standards?

records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §11.5, does your Software Quality Assurance Plan:
records.query: Provide for QA independence and for a conformity review before you declare compliance?

records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §11.6 through §11.8, do you have:
records.query: Requirements, design, and coding standards that set the rules and constraints your team will follow?

records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §11.6 through §11.8, do you have:
records.query: Coding standards appropriate to the actual programming language(s) you are using?

records.section: Section 6 — Making Sure the Plans Hold Together
records.subsection: A cross-check across all your plans:
records.query: Are the plans consistent with each other and with the PSAC — same processes, same life cycle, same software levels?

records.section: Section 6 — Making Sure the Plans Hold Together
records.subsection: A cross-check across all your plans:
records.query: Does the rigor in the plans match the software level (more verification and more independence at the higher levels)?
notes: This free checklist is a high-level sanity check — free to use and share. It doesn't include the item-level actions needed to fully close each objective; those live in AeroCert's comprehensive checklist, kept current as the standards change and available through donations. We'd love to hear how it worked for you — tell us at support@aerocert.org.
